text:
Market today: Risk-off tone with a chip-specific gut punch. Samsung and SK Hynix both cratering 9%+ means the semiconductor rout is global and accelerating - this hits your book directly. Margin environment is hostile right now.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the urgent one - it's a 3x leveraged chip ETF bleeding in exactly the sector getting hammered today; your stop is $200, price $217, gap risk is real on margin. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile - it's already near its $21.38 stop ($21.70 price), one bad tick away from triggering; margin amplifies this. SMH (-2.5%) is the unleveraged version of the same problem - watch it confirm whether chip weakness deepens.

Watch: 1) Does SMH hold $620 or roll over further - that tells you whether SOXL accelerates toward your stop. 2) Any U.S. chip names (TSM, ARM) catching contagion from the Korea selloff.

Bottom line: Protect capital on your leveraged losers today - SPAL and SOXL need your full attention before anything else.
plays: SPAL: At $21.70 with stop $21.38, you have almost no cushion - consider cutting now rather than waiting for the stop to trigger on a gap; leveraged ETF decay plus chip rout is a bad combo on margin.
SOXL: $217.55 with stop $200 - chip rout spreading globally today; consider tightening your stop or trimming size; a 3x levered name in a sector selloff on margin is where accounts blow up.
No new buys today - chip sector under active pressure, scanner setups like TSM and ARM are technically valid but buying into a global semiconductor rout on margin is the wrong timing.
